answer:
\boxed{F}

John Taylor of Caroline’s opposition to a national bank and his Anti-Federalist stance (fearing centralized power) align with his broader distrust of federal authority, including Congress’s ability to incorporate institutions like banks. His quote reflects his belief in states' rights and limited federal power, core Anti-Federalist principles. While he later defended slavery, his political identity is best tied to the Anti-Federalist tradition.
